courses.title: Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics
Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics

notes: The following transfer screening policy takes effect in fall 2024 and will be mandatory for all transfer students entering UC Santa Cruz fall 2026 and later. Transfer students entering UCSC prior to fall 2026 are encouraged to complete these requirements, but will still be considered for admission if they fulfill the 2023-2024 admissions screening requirements. Computer Science B.S. Transfer Admission Screening Policy Transfer students must complete the following five courses, or their articulated equivalents, with a combined minimum GPA of 3.0 or better by the end of the spring term prior to transferring in the fall: CSE 12 – Computer Systems and Assembly Language and Lab CSE 16 – Applied Discrete Mathematics CSE 30 – Programming Abstractions: Python MATH 19A – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics MATH 19B – Calculus for Science, Engineering, and Mathematics At least three of these five courses must be completed by the end of the fall term of the academic year preceding the student's planned entry to UC Santa Cruz, with a minimum 3.0 GPA in all completed Computer Science screening courses by that time. Any remaining screening courses must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer. Additional Information for Transfer Transfer students are strongly recommended to complete two of the preparatory courses listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section below, as well as most general education requirements prior to arriving at UC Santa Cruz. Transfer students who are not familiar with an object-oriented language may need to take a remedial course. Students familiar with C++ and Unix should find the transition to Python and C relatively simple. Please visit the Baskin Engineering Undergraduate Experience website to learn more about this major.

All transfer applicants must satisfy University of California admissions eligibility requirements and campus admission selection criteria. All admission requirements must be completed by the end of spring prior to transfer (see additional program specific deadlines listed below). For more information on UC admissions eligibility requirements and admission to UC Santa Cruz, please visit the Admissions website .

THIS IS A SCREENING MAJOR. Screening majors require the completion of specific preparatory courses and/or minimum GPA requirements. For detailed information about the screening requirements for this major, please refer to "Transfer Admission Screening Policy" below. Additional preparatory coursework listed in the “Recommended Preparation” section are not required for admission, but are strongly recommended. Completion of these courses will help ensure timely progress toward degree completion. More information and a list of screening majors and non-screening majors may be found on the Admissions website.

The UC Santa Cruz Advanced Placement (AP), International Baccalaureate (IB), and A-Level examination credit policies may be found in the UC Santa Cruz General Catalog . AP, IBH, and A-level exams may or may not satisfy course requirements, depending on the specific exam.
